title: Ike's Morning Digest
data_note: Built from publisher RSS metadata (titles + teaser text). `coverage_window_hours` is the default (40h); several feeds use a longer `rollingHours` (see `scripts/build-digest.mjs`) so rows align with what is still on each publisher’s listing pages. Commercial Observer keeps its retail category + hub supplement; TRD adds a New York–filtered global retail tag feed. PincusCo uses the live site feed only (`/category/retail/feed/` is stale). **CRE Daily** retail briefs come from `credaily.com/sectors/retail/feed/`. Open each link for the full article (subscriptions may apply in the browser).

section_overviews.commercial_observer:
Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear .

Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue.

Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group .

New Thai Restaurant to Open in 11K SF at 321 West 51st Street: One thing New York City doesn’t lack is a place to eat, and now there’s a new Thai restaurant from the restaurateurs behind Greenwich Village favorite Pranakhon coming to Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood.
section_overviews.new_york_post:
K-Pop company DearU to open flagship store in Union Square: DearU, a global K-Pop company, will bring its US flagship and its first permanent brick-and-mortar store, to 24 Union Square East. Newmark’s Jeffrey Roseman and Drew Weiss are the exclusive leasing agents for the property’s owners, the S.

LA Graffiti Tower developer has insidious plot to make insane cash before tackling eyesore: Insiders: A bankruptcy court decision on the sale has been pushed back again to July 20 as the prospective buyer scrambles to convince the city hall that they can clean up the infamous three, graffiti-tagged towers.

Final design for NYC Penn Station’s redevelopment one step closer — including how much it could cost: The pre-development phase will run through 2027, during which time Amtrak and the development partners said they will conduct “robust” community engagement before the final plan is completed.

Booming town of Silverwood where homes are $400K and buyers have to sign ‘kindness’ waiver: The 9,000-acre project first grabbed headlines for its relatively affordable home prices — starting in the low $400,000s.
section_overviews.pincusco:
Imperial Sterling pays $13M for retail in Carnegie Hill: Imperial Sterling through the entity Imperial Madison, LLC paid $13 million to Keith Kantrowitz and.

Gora Group pays $16.8M to Treeline Companies for office in Brooklyn Heights: Gora Group through the entity 188 Montague LLC paid $16.8 million to Treeline Companies through.

Ridwan Kabir pays $4.3M for office in Hollis: Ridwan Kabir through the entity 189 Hillside Plaza LLC paid $4.3 million to Shahid Ali.

Jacob Kohn pays $5M to Cycamore Capital for mixed-use in Bedford Stuyvesant: Jacob Kohn through the entity Myrtle Kent Development LLC paid $5 million to Cycamore Capital.
section_overviews.the_real_deal:
NYC’s top construction permits: Week ending June 19, 2026: New Building: The largest new building application filed last week was in Crotona Park East, where Luce Popaj plans to build two residential buildings at 1489 and 1495 Boston Road, spanning a combined 73,689 square feet. The buildings will house a total of 127 units and stand nine and 12 stories tall.

Kosher grocer expanding into Bedford-Stuyvesant: A kosher grocer is heading for Bedford-Stuyvesant. Bingo Wholesale acquired a three-parcel development site in the Brooklyn neighborhood for $50 million, the Commercial Observer reported.

Cracking the code: How to fix Long Island housing: Long Island is a poster child for New York’s housing problem. At its heart is a bad bargain: The state has bestowed it with an extensive commuter rail network, yet localities cling to single-family zoning.

Rosen roadblock? SL Green sues RFR over Madison Avenue building access: Is Aby Rosen holding up SL Green’s latest office project? Marc Holliday’s REIT is taking Rosen’s RFR Holding to court over access to an office building adjacent to its Madison Avenue development site.

items.headline: Fred Astaire Dance Studios Leases First Park Slope Location
items.summary: The international dance franchise Fred Astaire Dance Studios is waltzing into Brooklyn’s Park Slope neighborhood. The school’s new lease at 508 Fifth Avenue spans 2,500 square feet of ground-floor retail space previously occupied by shoe store Bobby Sportswear . The 10-year deal’s asking rent was $72 per square foot. The lease is the New York dance studio franchise’s sixth deal completed by Lee & Associates NYC ‘s Claude Kolb .…

items.headline: Cashmere Brand Kujten Picks Acadia’s 92 Greene Street for Second U.S. Location
items.summary: French cashmere brand Kujten has leased a second Manhattan location less than a year after opening its first U.S. flagship along Madison Avenue. The upcoming store at Acadia Realty Trust ’s 92 Greene Street , a midblock address bordered by Spring and Prince streets, sits in the heart of SoHo’s bustling shopping corridor. Kujten signed a long-term, 2,567-square-foot lease, with 1,531 square feet on the ground floor and 1,036 square feet on the lower level, according to landlord broker Newmark .…

items.headline: Bistro Pardon My French Sets Up Shop at Jeff Sutton’s 373 Broome Street
items.summary: Ooh la la. French-inspired bistro Pardon My French is coming to Manhattan’s SoHo neighborhood after signing a 1,000-square-foot lease at 373 Broome Street , a property owned in a joint venture between Jeff Sutton ’s Wharton Properties and Gorjian Real Estate Group, according to broker Meridian Capital Group . The asking rent for the ground-floor retail space was $225 per square foot. The length of the lease, which was signed at the end of April, was not disclosed.…

items.headline: New Thai Restaurant to Open in 11K SF at 321 West 51st Street
items.summary: One thing New York City doesn’t lack is a place to eat, and now there’s a new Thai restaurant from the restaurateurs behind Greenwich Village favorite Pranakhon coming to Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood. SG Thai , the hospitality group behind Pranakhon, located at 88 University Place near Union Square , will open a new eatery at Nicole Franques ’s 321 West 51st Street .…

items.headline: NYC’s top construction permits: Week ending June 19, 2026
items.summary: New Building: The largest new building application filed last week was in Crotona Park East, where Luce Popaj plans to build two residential buildings at 1489 and 1495 Boston Road, spanning a combined 73,689 square feet. The buildings will house a total of 127 units and stand nine and 12 stories tall. Nikolai Katz is the architect.…

items.headline: Content Creation Platform Spotter Takes 17K SF at Thor’s 25 West 39th Street
items.summary: Upload complete. Spotter , a platform that provides tools to help content creators post on YouTube , has signed a 17,000-square-foot lease at Thor Equities ’ 25 West 39th Street in Midtown. The lease spans the entire 14th floor of the 16-story office building one block south of Bryant Park , according to Crain’s New York Business , which first reported the deal. The deal is a three-year lease with an asking rent of approximately $70 per square foot.…

items.headline: TA Realty Buys Two Staten Island Retail Buildings for $79M
items.summary: Boston-based real estate investment firm TA Realty has purchased two buildings within Staten Island’s Tysens Park Shopping Center , according to property records made public Monday. The James Raisides -led TA Realty purchased the properties at 2712 Hylan Boulevard and 2754 Hylan Boulevard in New Dorp Beach, Staten Island, for a total of $79.2 million from the Bregman Organization , a deed transfer in property records indicates .…

items.headline: Design Firm Pink Sparrow Inks 62K-SF Deal in Long Island City
items.summary: Pink Sparrow , an experiential design and fabrication company, has relocated its headquarters to 30-10 Review Avenue in Long Island City, Queens, from its previous space about 1.5 miles away at 24 Greenpoint Avenue in Greenpoint, Brooklyn, Commercial Observer has learned. The design firm’s new space at the industrial property near Newton Creek spans 62,000 square feet, according to tenant broker Newmark .…

items.headline: Imperial Sterling pays $13M for retail in Carnegie Hill

items.headline: French Cafe L’Ami Pierre to Open at Midtown’s 400 Madison Avenue
items.summary: A French cafe by acclaimed restaurateur Pierre-Antoine Raberin is opening a second location in Midtown, Commercial Observer has learned. Raberin has signed a 2,400-square-foot lease at 400 Madison Avenue for the second outpost of L’Ami Pierre , according to tenant broker Judson CRE . The deal follows the exit of Raberin’s partner, Michelin -starred chef Eric Ripert , in May 2023 .…

items.headline: Kosher grocer expanding into Bedford-Stuyvesant
items.summary: A kosher grocer is heading for Bedford-Stuyvesant. Bingo Wholesale acquired a three-parcel development site in the Brooklyn neighborhood for $50 million, the Commercial Observer reported. The acquisition was made through the grocery store’s holding company, Norworth Holdings. The sellers of the three sites were a pair of entities linked to the Follman family. Members of the Follman family had been feuding in the courts, according to PincusCo, which held up the sale before it closed this month.…

items.headline: K-Pop company DearU to open flagship store in Union Square
items.summary: DearU, a global K-Pop company, will bring its US flagship and its first permanent brick-and-mortar store, to 24 Union Square East. Newmark’s Jeffrey Roseman and Drew Weiss are the exclusive leasing agents for the property’s owners, the S. Klein Family LLC.

items.headline: Blackstone Levels Up San Francisco Footprint With $279M Hyatt Regency Purchase
items.summary: “High on a hill, it calls to me,” Tony Bennett once sang of San Francisco, and he’s not the only one answering the enticing call of the City By the Bay. Blackstone just acquired the Hyatt Regency Embarcadero from Sunstone Hotel Investors for roughly $279 million, Commercial Observer can first report. The 821-key luxury waterfront hotel, at 5 Embarcadero Center , features a 17-story atrium and unobstructed views over the city and San Francisco Bay.…

items.summary: A bankruptcy court decision on the sale has been pushed back again to July 20 as the prospective buyer scrambles to convince the city hall that they can clean up the infamous three, graffiti-tagged towers.

items.headline: Cracking the code: How to fix Long Island housing
items.summary: Long Island is a poster child for New York’s housing problem. At its heart is a bad bargain: The state has bestowed it with an extensive commuter rail network, yet localities cling to single-family zoning. A 2023 analysis found an astonishing 89 percent of the buildable, residential land in Nassau and Suffolk counties was zoned for single-family. Another 8.5 percent allowed two-family homes. Just 3.6 percent was zoned for multifamily, meaning three or more units.…

items.headline: Rosen roadblock? SL Green sues RFR over Madison Avenue building access
items.summary: Is Aby Rosen holding up SL Green’s latest office project? Marc Holliday’s REIT is taking Rosen’s RFR Holding to court over access to an office building adjacent to its Madison Avenue development site. SL Green alleges RFR has ignored repeated requests for permission to enter its properties at 350 Madison Avenue and 10 East 45th Street, delaying progress on the developer’s planned office tower.…

items.summary: The 9,000-acre project first grabbed headlines for its relatively affordable home prices — starting in the low $400,000s.

items.headline: Medallion Financial Corp. moving to 667 Madison Ave.
items.summary: Taxi industry mogul Andrew Murstein hailed a ride to Leonard Stern’s 667 Madison Ave., joining an array of elite tenants at the boutique office tower.

items.headline: NY Dirt: Can’t pay, won’t pay
items.summary: “Rent collections are down in New York — and no one’s sure why.” That was a tweet from Politico earlier this week, one that got a good amount of attention. It was related to a story they published about affordable housing operators and data showing they are collecting less in rent. I similarly wrote about this subject for our January magazine. This is a pertinent issue that affects how affordable housing operators are able to stay in the black.…

items.headline: REBNY legislative lead resigns after tearing down political signs
items.summary: An act of campaign sabotage during primary voting Tuesday landed REBNY’s vice president of legislative affairs for New York in hot water. Dev Awasthi was filmed removing campaign signs for NY-12 Congressional candidate Jack Schlossberg from a streetlight post and throwing them in the trash earlier today. He no longer works for the real estate industry group.…

items.headline: NYC mom sues NYCHA claiming her 2 kids were bitten and ‘permanently injured’ by mice in her rodent-infested home
items.summary: Doris Montalvo claims in the suit that NYCHA had been put on notice about a rodent infestation at 38 Rutgers St. but refused to act.

items.headline: REBNY taps Fried Frank partner Jonathan Mechanic as new chair
items.summary: The Real Estate Board of New York has chosen attorney Jonathan Mechanic, a partner at Fried Frank who runs the firm’s real estate department, as its next chair. At the start of 2027 Mechanic will succeed current Chair Jed Walentas, CEO of Two Trees Management, REBNY announced Tuesday. “To be the first practicing attorney elected Chairman of REBNY in its storied history is one of the greatest honors of my career.…
